segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: Did the crew of the Elkhor find what they were looking for?

translations.zh-Hans: “麋鹿号”的船员找到他们要找的东西了吗？

segments.text: Why?

translations.zh-Hans: 为什么？

segments.text: The salvage operation had been a complete failure.

translations.zh-Hans: 这次打捞行动彻底失败了。

segments.text: The small ship, Elkhor, which had been searching the Barents Sea for weeks, was on its way home.

translations.zh-Hans: 小船“麋鹿号”在巴伦支海搜寻了数周后，正在返航。

segments.text: A radio message from the mainland had been received by the ship's captain instructing him to give up the search.

translations.zh-Hans: 船长收到大陆发来的无线电报，命令他放弃搜寻。

segments.text: The captain knew that another attempt would be made later, for the sunken ship he was trying to find had been carrying a precious cargo of gold bullion.

translations.zh-Hans: 船长知道以后还会再作尝试，因为他要寻找的沉船载有一批珍贵的金条。

segments.text: Despite the message, the captain of the Elkhor decided to try once more.

translations.zh-Hans: 尽管接到命令，“麋鹿号”船长还是决定再试一次。

segments.text: The seabed was scoured with powerful nets and there was a tremendous excitement on board when a chest was raised from the bottom.

translations.zh-Hans: 强力渔网在海床上来回搜索；当一只箱子从海底被打捞上来时，船上一片激动。

segments.text: Though the crew were at first under the impression that the lost ship had been found, the contents of the chest proved them wrong.

translations.zh-Hans: 起初，船员们以为找到了那艘失踪的船，但箱内的物品证明他们错了。

segments.text: What they had in fact found was a ship which had been sunk many years before.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们实际找到的是一艘多年前沉没的船。

segments.text: The chest contained the personal belongings of a seaman, Alan Fielding.

translations.zh-Hans: 箱子里装着一名叫艾伦·菲尔丁的水手的私人物品。

segments.text: There were books, clothing and photographs, together with letters which the seaman had once received from his wife.

translations.zh-Hans: 里面有书、衣服和照片，还有这名水手曾收到的妻子来信。

segments.text: The captain of the Elkhor ordered his men to salvage as much as possible from the wreck.

translations.zh-Hans: “麋鹿号”船长命令船员尽可能多地从沉船中打捞物品。

segments.text: Nothing of value was found, but the numerous items which were brought to the surface proved to be of great interest.

translations.zh-Hans: 虽然没有发现值钱的东西，但打捞上来的众多物品却十分有趣。

segments.text: From a heavy gun that was raised, the captain realized that the ship must have been a cruiser.

translations.zh-Hans: 从打捞上来的一门重炮判断，船长意识到这一定是一艘巡洋舰。

segments.text: In another chest, which contained the belongings of a ship's officer, there was an unfinished letter which had been written on March 14, 1943.

translations.zh-Hans: 另一个箱子装着一名海军军官的物品，其中有一封写于1943年3月14日、尚未写完的信。

segments.text: The captain learnt from the letter that the name of the lost ship was the Karen.

translations.zh-Hans: 船长从信中得知，这艘沉船名叫“卡伦号”。

segments.text: The most valuable find of all was the ship's log book.

translations.zh-Hans: 所有发现中最有价值的是航海日志。

segments.text: Parts of which it was still possible to read.

translations.zh-Hans: 其中有些部分仍然可以辨认。

segments.text: From this the captain was able to piece together all the information that had come to light.

translations.zh-Hans: 船长据此拼凑出了所有已经查明的信息。

segments.text: The Karen had been sailing in a convoy to Russia when she was torpedoed by an enemy submarine.

translations.zh-Hans: “卡伦号”在船队护航下驶往俄国时，被一艘敌方潜艇的鱼雷击中。

segments.text: This was later confirmed by a naval official at the Ministry of Defense after the Elkhor had returned home.

translations.zh-Hans: “麋鹿号”返航后，国防部的一名海军官员证实了这一点。

segments.text: All the items that were found were sent to the war museum.

translations.zh-Hans: 所有打捞上来的物品都被送进了战争博物馆。
